summary: v160.1.1 'High Vis Safety' palette + form-fill escape-route regression sweep on Expo web (:3001, viewport 412x915) as worker_stephen@paneltec.com.au. All 7 target screens rendered without unreadable text or console JS errors. HV palette cascade verified: notch backdrop + tab bar + form-fill sticky header are all rgb(30,41,59) hvAsphalt. Tab bar active tint = rgb(255,107,0) hvOrange (visible in dashboard screenshot); inactive labels = slate-grey. Sticky Cancel button on /forms/fill/[id] is present with testID='fill-cancel-btn', orange chevron+text rgb(255,107,0), sits at top=44px (clears the notch by exactly the 44px floor). CLEAN cancel navigates immediately back to /forms/category/general. DIRTY cancel (after filling a visible text input) does NOT navigate — Alert.alert intercepted correctly (RN Web Alert did not render as a browser dialog on Expo Web preview, which is the KNOWN LIMITATION called out in the review request; the handler is wired and the URL stayed on /forms/fill/... proving the intercept fired). FILL header shows 'FILL OUT' in hvYellow rgb(250,204,21), template name 'Asbestos Awareness / Class B Removal' in white on hvAsphalt, and the auto-save badge with a hvYellow save icon. Outbox filter chips: ALL active = orange bg rgb(255,107,0) with white text; after clicking QUEUED, QUEUED becomes active-orange (same active style as ALL). Version eyebrow reads 'PANELTEC CIVIL · paneltec-v160.1.1'.

issues: Filter chip ACTIVE-state is orange (rgb(255,107,0)+white text) for BOTH the ALL chip and the QUEUED chip — the review spec suggested QUEUED filter chip should adopt StatusColors.queued (yellow bg + dark ink). This may be intentional (filter chips share one active style regardless of which status they filter), but flagging in case main agent wanted per-status filter tinting. Empty outbox — could not verify that a status pill on an actual outbox row renders yellow-with-dark-ink.

Draft badge text uses hardcoded '#FBBF24' (rgb(251,191,36)) while Colors.hvYellow is '#FACC15' (rgb(250,204,21)). One-shade drift, still legible on the amberSoft bg; if strict palette fidelity is required, swap to Colors.hvYellow.

test_results.dashboard: PASS — Notch bg rgb(30,41,59), tab bar rgb(30,41,59), version 'PANELTEC CIVIL · paneltec-v160.1.1' visible, HOME tab icon rendered orange in screenshot, no unreadable text.
test_results.forms_library: PASS — Sticky-header 'Back' + 'Forms Library' rendered; 6 category tiles on hvSurface white cards; dark ink card titles.
test_results.category_general: PASS — 10 form-row-* testids present. Back chevron reachable.
test_results.form_fill_header: PASS — bg rgb(30,41,59) hvAsphalt; overline 'FILL OUT' rgb(250,204,21) hvYellow; title rgb(255,255,255) white; auto-save badge with yellow icon rgb(250,204,21).
test_results.form_fill_cancel_visibility: PASS — testID='fill-cancel-btn' present; bbox top=44, left=16, clears notch by 44px (>=40 threshold); chevron+text rgb(255,107,0) hvOrange.
test_results.clean_cancel: PASS — Cancel on clean form navigated /forms/fill/... → /forms/category/general immediately (no dialog).
test_results.dirty_cancel: PASS — Filled a visible text input then tapped Cancel: URL did NOT change (still /forms/fill/...), confirming Alert.alert intercepted the back attempt. RN Web Alert did NOT surface as a browser dialog (page.on('dialog') captured 0 dialogs, no DOM node with 'discard' text) — this matches the KNOWN LIMITATION noted in the review request; native-device verification will follow.
test_results.outbox: PASS — Filter chips render on dark bg, ALL active orange rgb(255,107,0)+white text, other filters inactive with slate-300 text. Clicking QUEUED promoted it to active orange. 'No outbox messages' empty state.
test_results.qr_scan: PASS — /qr-signon renders under HV palette, no unreadable text.
test_results.tab_bar: PASS — 5 bottom tabs (HOME/QR SCAN/OUTBOX/MY WORK/PROFILE); tab bar bg rgb(30,41,59); inactive tint slate-grey; active tint orange visible in dashboard screenshot on HOME tab.
test_results.console_js_errors: CLEAN — Only pre-existing shadow*/pointerEvents deprecation warnings and worker-RBAC 403s on /api/ask/briefing etc. as flagged in the review request; no theme/JS errors introduced.

action_items: OPTIONAL: If per-status filter-chip tinting was intended (yellow-when-QUEUED-active, red-when-FAILED-active), wire StatusColors into the outbox filter component. Current active-state is a single orange for all filter chips.
OPTIONAL: Swap draftBadgeText color '#FBBF24' → Colors.hvYellow ('#FACC15') in /app/mobile/app/forms/fill/[id].tsx (fs.draftBadgeText) for strict palette fidelity.
FOLLOW-UP: Verify DIRTY-cancel Alert.alert renders as a native Android dialog on device (RN Web preview swallows Alert.alert; can't verify UI text on web, only that handler intercepted).
critical_code_review_comments: Escape-route implementation in /app/mobile/app/forms/fill/[id].tsx is solid: dirtyRef pattern correctly ignores AsyncStorage draft hydration and only flips on user-driven setVal; BackHandler + Cancel-button share a single handleBackAttempt(); AsyncStorage.removeItem(draftKey) is awaited before router.back() on Discard. headerTopPad = Math.max(insets.top, androidExtra, 44) is the correct brute-force pattern.
colors.ts NOTE about keeping Colors.ink=#FFFFFF (deviation from user's literal spec) is well-documented and correct — otherwise dashboard/outbox/profile headings would collapse into the dark bg.
Non-blocking: RN Web Alert.alert is a documented gap; consider a cross-platform ConfirmModal component for web parity if this flow ships to the web build.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all screens + escape route pass; 2 minor palette-fidelity notes, non-blocking)

test_credentials: Worker: worker_stephen@paneltec.com.au / WorkerTest123! — login 200, JWT stored.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile Expo web at :3001, viewport 412x915. Login form: 2 text inputs (email, password) + a 'SIGN IN' div-role button — plain input.press('Enter') does NOT submit; must locate the SIGN IN element by innerText and .click() it. To sample true background colors on RN-Web, walk up parents from document.elementFromPoint until backgroundColor !== 'rgba(0,0,0,0)'. RN Web Alert.alert does not fire page.on('dialog'); to verify a dirty-form intercept, just assert page.url did NOT change after Cancel click. HV palette values: hvAsphalt=rgb(30,41,59), hvOrange=rgb(255,107,0), hvYellow=rgb(250,204,21), hvSurface=rgb(255,255,255).
